steps.title: Recognize this as a direct substitution problem
steps.content: We have the equation $s = 40 + 2t$ where $s$ is speed in mph and $t$ is time in seconds. We need the speed when $t = 4$ seconds.

steps.title: Substitute and calculate the speed
steps.content:
Substitute $t = 4$ into the equation:

$$s = 40 + 2(4) = 40 + 8 = 48$$

The speed is 48 miles per hour. That matches choice D — D is correct.
